sanskrit: सर्वकर्माणि मनसा सन्न्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी। नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन्॥
transliteration: sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukhaṁ vaśī / nava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan
literal_meaning: All actions—by the mind—having renounced—he dwells—in happiness—master; in the nine-gated city—the embodied—never—acting—nor causing to act.
contextual_meaning: The psychologically integrated person observes bodily impulses without identifying with them, experiencing inner peace while the body-mind complex continues its automatic functions.
psychological_state: Observing-ego position: witnessing automatic thoughts/urges without fusion or suppression.
trigger_scenario: You’re stuck in traffic, late for a meeting; instead of gripping the wheel and cursing, you notice the tension, breathe, and let the body drive while the mind stays calm.
anchor_text: Watch the body act while the mind rests in calm detachment.
